file_insights.changes: Added 4 sequential validation checks to post_cards_new handler after path separator validation
key_additions: Allow-list validation: only alphanumeric, dash, underscore, dot
file_insights.validation_order: Specific bad patterns first, then allow-list approach

file_insights.changes: Added 4 new test cases for ID validation
file_insights.test_coverage: Each validation rule has dedicated negative test; positive case validates acceptance

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple sequential checks: path separators → path traversal → encoding chars → null bytes → allow-list
patterns_discovered.benefit: Explicit error messages for each rejection reason aids debugging and security auditing

patterns_discovered.description: Test and reject known attack vectors before applying broad character restrictions
patterns_discovered.implementation: Individual checks for '..' and '%' before final alphanumeric allow-list

patterns_discovered.description: Each validation rule has at least one negative test case verifying rejection
patterns_discovered.coverage: Valid character set test ensures allow-list doesn't over-restrict

patterns_discovered.description: Character validation uses positive assertion (what IS allowed) not exclusion (what is NOT allowed)
patterns_discovered.rationale: Allow-lists are more secure and maintainable than deny-lists

gotchas_discovered.context: Dot is valid in filenames (e.g., version numbering: test-v1.2) but '..' is a path traversal vector
gotchas_discovered.solution: Separate check for '..' pattern preserves dot utility while blocking traversal

gotchas_discovered.context: Path separator check (existing) must run before allow-list to catch '/' early
gotchas_discovered.observation: New checks placed after path separator validation maintains correct precedence

gotchas_discovered.context: Tests expect both status code (400) AND specific error message in response body
gotchas_discovered.implementation: Each rejection returns structured JSON with descriptive error string

gotchas_discovered.context: While rare in HTTP bodies, null bytes can truncate strings in C-interop or filesystem operations
gotchas_discovered.solution: Explicit null byte check provides defense for downstream zam/dub interaction

approach_outcome.security_boundary: Card ID sanitization now rejects: './', '../', '%XX', null bytes, non-alphanumeric
approach_outcome.impact: Prevents path traversal, URL decoding attacks, and filesystem abuse via card creation endpoint

recommendations.action: Document allowed character set in API specification
recommendations.rationale: Clients need clear guidance; currently only discoverable from error messages or code

recommendations.action: Add length constraint on card ID (e.g., max 128 chars)
recommendations.rationale: Unbounded IDs could cause filesystem or database issues; aligns with filesystem path limits

recommendations.action: Consider rate limiting on validation failures
recommendations.rationale: Repeated failed creation attempts could indicate scanning or attack; not critical for MVP

recommendations.action: Audit other POST /cards endpoints for similar injection vectors
recommendations.rationale: validate spec field length/content encoding; template field same checks
recommendations.related_code: req.template and req.spec fields in post_cards_new handler

recommendations.action: Consider UUID or URL-safe base64 encoding for card IDs
recommendations.rationale: Would eliminate allow-list maintenance if adopting stricter format in future waves
